title: [Bug]: Oracle MySQL SQL builder should keep Bandit suppressions on flagged lines
summary: The Oracle MySQL SQL builder uses strict identifier allow-listing and bind values, but one reviewed dynamic SQL expression places the Bandit suppression on the assignment line instead of the f-string line Bandit reports.
observed: The full repository check failed during the Bandit step with B608 on the Oracle MySQL upsert SQL builder even though the query values are bound and identifiers are validated.
expected: Reviewed Bandit suppressions for validated dynamic SQL should be attached to the exact line Bandit evaluates so the security check remains deterministic.
reproduction: Run the repository check script after adding the Oracle MySQL SQL builder.
failing_test: Add a focused unit test that inspects the Oracle MySQL SQL builder source and requires the upsert f-string line to carry both the Ruff and Bandit review annotations.
impact: The defect blocks release validation and could encourage ad hoc suppression changes if it is not documented and tested.
delivery_semantics: The defect is limited to static-analysis annotation placement. It does not alter SQL values, sink writes, transaction commits, ACK ordering, retries, or DLQ behavior.
security: The fix keeps the existing secure SQL construction model: table and column identifiers are allow-list validated, and row values remain driver bind parameters. The suppression documents a reviewed false positive rather than hiding unvalidated input.
acceptance: The Oracle MySQL upsert SQL f-string line carries the reviewed Bandit B608 suppression.
A focused regression test protects the annotation placement.
Bandit passes as part of the full repository check.
The Oracle MySQL SQL unit tests continue to pass.
tests: Run the focused Oracle MySQL SQL tests, the new annotation regression test, and the full repository check script.
documentation: No user-facing documentation changes are required beyond changelog and test-report evidence.
closeout: Mark completed after the annotation regression test and full repository check pass with sanitized evidence.
